title: Vaughania perrieri
taxonomy: Vaughania perrieri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It is part of the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Millettieae. This plant is native to Madagascar and is a member of the genus Vaughania, which is a small group of flowering plants. The genus Vaughania is closely related to the genus Millettia, with both being part of the larger Fabaceae family. Vaughania perrieri is a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Vaughania perrieri, also known as Piper nigrum var. perrieri or the Madagascar pepper, is a species of flowering plant in the pepper family (Piperaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Vaughania perrieri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, such as piperine, quercetin, and ellagic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
